no: EE version of Dark Horizons. Should install after EET Start on BG2EE — not pre-EET. See i=573 (legacy tp2 version) for notes.

cl.m: Added from Infinity Insanity WeiDU logs

conflicts.reason: Both mods modify shared BG1 area scripts. Compatible if Dark Horizons is installed BEFORE SotSC (per SotSC readme). SotSC expects Dark Horizons changes to already be present.
Generally compatible. Standard install order: Dark Horizons first, BG1 NPC Project after. Both add BG1 content but don't directly conflict.
Same mod content under two tp2 names (legacy vs EE packaging). Install only one version.
conflicts.source: Same GitHub repo (The-Gate-Project/DarkHorizons), single tp2 with GAME_IS checks, 2026-03

sum: Adds a BG1 quest chain involving mysterious dark forces threatening the Sword Coast. Several hours of new content with new areas and encounters.

co.n: "Dark Horizons" Mod for Baldur's Gate: Enhanced Edition
co.no: Older versions of Saradas Magic (pre-v1.2) conflict with this mod due to area script overwriting. Current versions are compatible. mod_installer: game detection fails if installed pre-EET — ELSE-IF chain (bgee → eet → tutu → totsc → bgt → FAIL) correctly includes eet at line 19, but requires EET already installed so GAME_IS eet evaluates true. When run on BGEE before EET, installs as bgee which is valid. When run post-EET, GAME_IS eet is detected. Fix: install post-EET on BG2EE executable. The recorded failure suggests it was attempted pre-EET on BGEE, or eet_cpmvars.tpa (line 23) was missing. Confirm ph is BG1 content installed in EET phase.
